heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: A storm is passing through the area.  This marks the first storm since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 7th since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 9 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 23 December 2025.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is no heatwaves at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 heatwavess, with an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (197°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 8.0°C), outside temperature (down 4.9°C), and feels like temperature (down 4.0°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 290 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.7°C), outside temperature (up 8.9°C), cloud levels (up 330 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 290.3m lower than yesterday, 330.0m lower than the past week, 28.3m higher than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 828m and a peak of 2280m, averaging 990.6m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; icy, freezing temperatures. Also, dry air conditions likely prevail and calm and still conditions are expected. Showers, partial cloudiness with some periods of sun, and moderate levels of sunlight will be present. High pressure suggests settled weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-4.9°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
